lines.text: Boot sequence... hello! I'm Patch. I'm what's left of the World Bridge's maintenance system.
...The bridge outside is in a thousand pieces.
One thousand and seven, actually. I counted. It's all I've done for a week.
Here's the plan: you run the item shop, we fund repairs, and we reconnect all seven gates. Easy!
Seven gates. One shop. Thirty-five days.
I said 'easy', not 'reasonable'. First gate: Kingdom Hearts. It's the cheap one!
Gate One: Traverse Town. Corruption reading: mild-to-embarrassing.
Recover the World Shard from the boss, and save 10,000 gold. Shop's this way!
Whoa, a shop between worlds! How much for the potion?
Let's say... 80 gold.
Deal! Shopkeepers never overcharge!
He didn't even blink. Hero, we could have said EIGHT HUNDRED.
Patch.
I'm just saying the boy has no defensive economics whatsoever.
Got it! One World Shard! It was inside the Fat Bandit the whole time.
Do NOT tell me where exactly. Shard acquired! Now we just need the repair fee.
Gate One: ONLINE. Traverse Town is connected!
The lamplight comes all the way through the gate.
...I forgot bridges could look like that. Okay! Gate Two! The Mushroom one!
Gate Two: Mushroom Kingdom. Repair estimate: 25,000. The economy over there is coin-based. Literally. Coins float in the air.
Sounds profitable.
Sounds UNREGULATED.
Welcome! Now, our store operates a tiered pricing model with dynamic market adjustments—
(Thumbs-up.)
—and per-franchise fee structures, plus a loyalty program—
(Two thumbs-up.)
Hero, is he agreeing or has he simply run out of thumbs?
(Holds up the World Shard triumphantly.) Oh yeah!
The King Bob-omb just... wanted a hug? A big, explosive hug? Anyway. SHARD GET.
Gate Two: ONLINE. Warp pipes are now a valid commuting option.
Two down.
Five to go. The next one has ambient orchestral music and unresolved feelings.
Gate Three: the Crystal Ruins. 60,000 gold. The residents are dramatic and their swords are enormous.
Bigger swords, bigger margins.
You're learning. I'm so proud and slightly concerned.
...Nice corner.
You can stand in it, if you want.
I don't need anything. I'll take the corner.
Sir, the corner is not for sale. Sir, please stop brooding near the ice cream.
Behemoth's down. It had... crossover attacks. It threw a Poké Ball at me.
That's bridge corruption for you. Worlds leaking into worlds. Shard secured. Three of seven!
Gate Three: ONLINE. Crystal resonance nominal. Chocobo migration patterns restored.
...Not bad, shopkeeper.
HE SPOKE. Hero, mark the calendar.
Gate Four: Hyrule. 120,000. Fair warning: the hero there communicates exclusively via meaningful silences.
We'll get along fine.
That is EXACTLY what worries me.
Uh-huh. The boomerang's 400, but for you, 350.
Fine. 325, but only because of what happened with the chickens.
WHAT happened with the chickens?! I'm the interface here! INTERFACE ME!
... (Holds up the World Shard. A familiar jingle almost plays.)
Four shards! Also, Gohma's eye is NOT merchandise, please put it down.
Gate Four: ONLINE. The Lost Woods have stopped rearranging our street signs.
Halfway there.
Statistically past halfway! Emotionally, who can say.
Gate Five: Hidden Leaf. 225,000 gold. Their economy runs on ramen and determination.
Which one do we stock?
Both. OBVIOUSLY.
I need that summoning scroll! It's 1,200?! Okay. OKAY. I have... 12 gold.
That's one percent.
AND a coupon for Ichiraku! AND my eternal gratitude! Future Hokage gratitude!
Hero, the gratitude of a Hokage is not a recognized currency. ...Yet. Hmm.
Zabuza's back to normal! He says sorry about the sword thing! Sort of! With his eyebrows!
Shard five! And tell Zabuza the cleaver aisle restocks on Thursdays.
Gate Five: ONLINE. Warning: ramen demand has tripled. This is a good problem.
BELIEVE IT!
He can't hear the difference between me and the till anymore, can he.
Gate Six: The Lookout. 400,000 gold. The locals can destroy planets, so, uh, keep receipts.
Noted.
And if a very cheerful man orders 'everything', get payment UP FRONT.
Wow, a food shelf! I'll take all of it! And whatever a 'senzu markup' is, one of those too!
That'll be 41,300 gold.
...Ha! Haha. So here's the thing about my other gi.
CODE ORANGE. IT'S HAPPENING. THE WALLET STORY. EVERYONE STAY CALM.
Vegeta's back to normal size! He says thanks!
Well, he said 'hmph', but it was a thankful 'hmph'.
Shard six! One gate left. The little yellow one.
Gate Six: ONLINE. The Lookout is back in the sky where it belongs.
Hey Hero! When this is over, let's spar! Or eat! Or both at once!
Both at once is how we lost the old food court, Son Goku.
Final gate: 700,000 gold. Deep breath. We've come a long way from selling ice cream to a boy with zero market sense.
He's a valued repeat customer.
He's our ENTIRE profit margin and I love him.
Pika.
It's proposing a 12% volume discount contingent on seasonal demand.
Pika-pi. Chuu.
NOW it's citing precedent! From OUR OWN LEDGER! Hero, hire this mouse.
Mewtwo has stood down. It thought the Crossroads was another laboratory. Another cage.
And now?
Now it's seen the shop. Cages don't have loyalty programs. SHARD SEVEN, HERO. THAT'S ALL OF THEM.
Gate Seven: ONLINE. All gates connected. Full bridge integrity in three... two...
...Hero. Something else just connected. Something that was never given a gate.
The Fade.
It's not attacking the gates. It's coming HERE. To the Crossroads.
The Null Archive. It's made of... everything. Deleted saves. Cancelled sequels. Worlds nobody visits anymore.
The Fade didn't break the bridge out of hate.
No. It broke the bridge because the bridge was never built for THEM. I checked the original blueprints, Hero. The forgotten worlds were excluded. On purpose.
Then we don't destroy the Fade. We renovate.
...I have never been prouder to be a maintenance system.
It's... quiet. The Fade is just standing there. It's holding a save file like it's a photograph.
Patch. Open a new gate. An eighth gate.
There's no blueprint for an eighth gate.
Then we're overdue. Every world gets a way home. Even the forgotten ones. Especially the forgotten ones.
...Drafting blueprint now. It's shaped like a shop door.
Gate Eight: OPEN. Permanent. Unconditional.
So the new worlds... can they visit the shop too?
First purchase is on the house.
WAIT, THAT'S ALLOWED?!
I've been PAYING?!
Pika.
It says 'welcome home', everyone. ...It says welcome home.
The gate destabilized... the connection collapsed. We missed the deadline.
Then we start the chapter over. We keep what we learned.
Rolling back to the start of the chapter. Your experience, friendships and know-how come with us. Pick a few things to keep — the rest returns to the bridge.
Hero? When the last gate opens... do maintenance systems get retired?
This one gets a shelf in the shop.
A DISPLAY shelf? Window-facing?! ...I would like that. Logging off happy.
Cloud stood in his corner for six hours today. Goku ate near him for three. Naruto talked at him for two.
Did Cloud leave?
No. And he bought THREE combo meals. I think that's friendship. I'm cataloguing it as friendship.
Data point: the Crossroads used to be a crossroads. People passed THROUGH it.
And now?
Now they stay. I have seventeen loitering complaints filed against a princess, a ninja, and one (1) electric mouse. I'm framing them.
Campaign complete! The bridge holds. The shop stands. And travelers keep coming.
Then we keep the lights on.
Endless Mode engaged! Same shop, infinite days, and the rent is now emotionally significant.
